title: Synthetic Identity Credit Builder with Planned Bust-Out After 90-Day Seasoning
narrative: Fraudster establishes synthetic identity with manufactured credit profile through strategic payment behavior over 90-120 days before bust-out. Account opens with small purchases ($15-$45) at grocery and gas stations (MCC 5411, 5541) with same-day or next-day full payments to build positive history. After 75-90 days of this pattern, card1_txn_count_24h jumps from baseline 1-2 to 8-12 transactions totaling $3,500-$8,000 across electronics retailers (MCC 5732, 5734) and digital goods platforms (ProductCD='W'). Velocity signatures show TransactionAmt_mean shifts from $28 during seasoning to $640 during bust-out window. P_emaildomain often free provider created within 6 months of first transaction. Final phase shows 15-25 transactions over 48-72 hours with declining authorization rates before account abandonment and inevitable chargebacks.
indicators: Initial 75-90 day period showing card1_txn_count_7d consistently 2-4 with TransactionAmt range $15-$45 and payment_within_24h flag=1 on 85%+ transactions
Sudden velocity spike where card1_txn_count_24h increases from historical mean 1.2 to 8+ transactions
TransactionAmt_mean increases 20x+ from seasoning period baseline (e.g., $28 to $640+) within 48-hour window
MCC shift from low-risk categories (5411, 5541, 5814) to high-risk electronics/digital goods (5732, 5734, ProductCD='W')
P_emaildomain age less than 180 days with free provider (gmail.com, yahoo.com) and addr1 PO box or commercial mail receiving agency
Final 48-72 hour burst showing 15-25 transactions with authorization decline rate climbing from 0% to 40%+ as limits exhaust
card1_amt_zscore spikes to 3.5+ standard deviations above account historical mean during bust-out phase
summary: Account builds trust over 75-90 days with small, immediately-paid purchases at groceries and gas stations, then suddenly shifts to 8-12 high-value electronics transactions within 24-48 hours showing 20x increase in average transaction amount and climbing decline rates.
reasoning: The fraudster is deliberately establishing credibility with responsible payment behavior to increase credit limits, then executing a coordinated bust-out where they max out available credit on easily-resold goods before abandoning the synthetic identity entirely.
indicator_explanations: Account shows deliberate trust-building with small purchases and prompt payments for three months
Transaction frequency suddenly explodes from steady low-volume to frantic burst activity
Purchase amounts skyrocket twenty-fold overnight from established small-dollar pattern
Spending pivots from groceries and gas to high-value electronics and digital goods
Email account is recent with free provider tied to mail drop address
Final surge shows repeated attempts as card approaches limit with mounting declines
Transaction sizes become extreme outliers compared to the account's entire history
